    Visit the pageBhagvadgita displayed in multi-language scripts

  • Bhagvadgita Audio links (mostly downloadable MP3s or online) : vaishnava.cz, all shlokas one by one in Gita supersite and its Beta version, gitamrta.org, Swami Tapasyanada's English translation read by Mike Scherer, Chinmaya-Chicago Chanting, Coordinated audio and text mantraonline.com, gitainternational.com, Gita foundation, yatharthgeeta.com.

  • A survey of gitas in Sanskrit literature, a PhD thesis by S. V. Oka, Savitribai Phule Pune University, 1956.
  • Various Gita Commentaries in downlodable PDF format. The commentaries include shA.nkarabhAShya, its vyAkhyA by Anandagiri, rAmAnujabhAShya, ve.nkaTanAtha's tAtparyachandrikA, AnandatIrtha's mAdhvabhAShya, jayatIrtha's prameyadIpikA, ve.nkaTanAtha's bramAnandagiryAkhyavyAkhyAna, vallabhAchArya;s tattvadIpikA, puruShottamajI's amR^itataraN~giNI, and nIlaka.nTha's bhAratabhAvadIpaH. It is a treasure for a serious student of Bhagvadgita. Download only the chapter you are going to study to save on bandwidth. A list of other commentaries with links is available on the Hindu dharma forum.

  • Critical editions of Gita, Belvalkar Gita, and text version.
    BORI Belvalkar DJVU, BORI Belvalkar PDF. Comparison of variations from Belvalkar's with the text available in standard form is in highlighted differnces file.

  • http://www.geetarahasya.com/ hosts Bhagvadgita text and translation in various languages such as Sanskrit, Assamee, Bangali, Hindi, Gujarati, Kannada, Malyalam, Marathi, Oriya, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu, Chinese, Persian, Norweigan, Dutch, German, Russian, Nepali, French. Additional sections include Books on Gita, related articles, audio section et cetera.

  • Amaithiyum Anandamum அமைதியும் ஆநந்தமும், Tamil Version of Bhagwat Gita titled Amithiyum Anandamum (Peace and joy), All the 701 slogas of Bhagwat Gita in Tamil Literature, Tamizh songs by Swami Krishnathmananda Saraswathi, Sakatapuram, Karnataka
    Swamiji has blessed us with these three E-book volumes covering six chapters each for the benefit of Tamil knowing persons who is in need of knowing Bhagwat Gita. It is made freely downloadable for seekers of divine spiritual knowledge. (First edition: 2023, Revised : September 2025. For suggestions and corrections, write to Shri K. Balachandran omsivakbc at yahoo.co.in).
          • AmaithiyumAnandamum Volume 1 (7.2M)
          • AmaithiyumAnandamum Volume 2 (7.5M)
          • AmaithiyumAnandamum Volume 3 (7.8M)
    See Message on E-book by SwamiKrishnathmanandaSaraswathi. Swamiji has since thoroughly revised his other work's 2nd edition as E-book viz. JnanaSitharal -titled in Tamil as ஞானச்சிதறல். This is a condensed Tamil version of Srimad Bhagwad Gita of all its 18 chapters. In other words, this ebook in Tamil provides essence of Srimad Bhagwad Gita. While Amaithiyum Anandamum is a word by word composition of all 701 verses of Srimad Bhagwad Gita as Tamil Poem, Jnana Sitharal is a composition of only the Essence of the Gita as Tamil Poem in his own way. Read a brief introduction of the author Swami Krishnathmananda Saraswathi.

  • Bhagavadgita As_It_Is by Swami Prabhupada, Original authorized Macmillan edition 1972. Explore other books at http://prabhupadabooks.com.

  • Bhagvadgita in Urdu Shayari

    • Source known/unknown but text is referred (?) or available (chaubis GitA, lakShmin\ArayaNa samhitA)
      1. Adbhuta or Nanaka Gita -- Translation of Japji of Guru Nanak -- Published from Calcutta in 1946 in Bengali script available at DLI
      2. Asmaka Gita -- Mahabharata, Santiparva, Rajadharma, chapter 28 ??
      3. Ashmaka Gita -- In the mokShasAmrAjyalakShmItantra ??
      4. Atmadarsana Gita -- By Buddhisagara (Jain), 182 stanzas -- Modern
      5. Balayogini Gita -- lakShmInArAyaNasaMhitA chaturtha khaNDa adhyAya 9-22
      6. Bhagavati Gita -- A text refers it to be Devi gita
      7. Bhutanatha Gita -- Gita by Lord Dharma Sastha (Ayyappa). It is a conversation between Lord Manikanta and Rajashekara Pandya, the pandala raja. It is a part of Bhoothanathopakhyanam that is published by Shri Maha Sasthru Seva Sangam in Sanskrit, English and Tamil. Contact V.Aravind Subramanyam at http://shanmatha.blogspot.com and www.aravindsastha.com. The Sangam is at Thejovathy, 94 B, Third Street, Tatabad, Coimbatore 641012 Ph:(0)99946 41801 Email: aravindsai at gmail.com
      8. Bodhananda Gita -- Summary of 10 Upanishads by Bodhananda Yajvan
      9. Brihaspati Gita -- (Chaubis Gita)
      10. Dayudo Gita (David Gita) -- Sanskrit Translation of Psalms of David by W Yates -- Published from Calcutta in 1844
      11. Devi Gita -- Kurma Purana, Chapter 12
      12. Garbha Gita -- Bhagavata?
      13. Griha Gita -- lakShmInArAyaNasaMhitA chaturtha khaNDa adhyAya 86-89
      14. Guru Gita 3 -- Rudrayamala Tantra --
      15. Jajali Gita -- Chaubis Gita
      16. (Not a Gita) Janaki Gita -- by Haryyacharya described as a musical drama based on Ramayana , see search references Critical Perspectives on the Ramayana and Dictionary Of Indology
      17. Japaka Gita -- Chaubis Gita
      18. Kapila Gita 2 -- Ascribed to Padma Purana but not found in it -- Siva-Paravati samvada in 5 chapters
      19. Karma Gita -- Ascribed to Padma Purana but not found in it --
      20. Kavasheya Gita -- Ascribed to Brahma Purana, but not found in it -- Vyasa-Sanatkumara Samvada (Granthappura p6)
      21. Khristu Gita -- -- Translation of 14 chapters of the Bible
      22. Kumara Gita by Puttaraja Kavi Gawai (1964) expounding Vira Saiva philosophy
      23. Lomasha Gita -- lakShmInArAyaNasaMhitA dvitIya khaNDa adhyAya 249-262
      24. Maheshvara Gita -- Chaubis Gita
      25. Manu Gita -- Chaubis Gita
      26. Markandeya Gita -- Chaubis Gita
      27. Narayana Gita -- By Nilakantha, Ramananda Sect -- Published by Govardhan Das in 1890 (DLI)???
      28. Panchashikha Gita -- Chaubis Gita
      29. Rama Gita 2 -- From Tattvasarayana -- Published by Adyar Library
      30. Rama Gita 3 -- Skanda Purana, Nirvana Khanda
      31. SanatkumAragItA Bhagavata IV:22 Teaching of Sanaka and his 3 brothers to King Prithu (meaning)
      32. SanatkumAra Gita 17 chapters of yAj~navalkya ?
      33. Sanatsujatiya Gita Mahabharat Udyogaparva 41-46 also known as Sanatsujatiya available at major works
      34. Saumya Gita -- Ascribed to Padma Purana but not found in it -- ??
      35. Savitri Gita -- Chaubis Gita
      36. Siva Gita -- Ascribed to Padma Purana but not found in it -- Published by Nirnaya Sagar, Khemraj, etc ??
      37. Sivarama Gita -- Part of a text named 'Sukodaya' or 'Advaitasudharasa'
      38. Sivottara Gita -- Ascribed to Harivamsa Purana but not found in it -- ??
      39. Sridhisa Gita -- -- Published by Bharata Dharma Mahamandal in 1920
      40. Tattva Gita -- Published in Gujarati from Ahmedabad in 1911 ?? medical tact in 18 Chapters
      41. Vadhu Gita -- lakShmInArAyaNasaMhitA tR^itIya khaNDa adhyAya 40-67
      42. Vedanta Gita -- -- Supporting Nimbarka matam
      43. Yogayaj~navalkya Gita -- same as Yoga Yajnavalkya Samhita (Available for encoding if interested) teaching dialogue between the sage Yajnavalkya and his wife, Gargi. 12 chapters, ~ 500 shlokas.

    • Many of these Gitas are not yet printed but are preserved in manuscript form in libraries in different parts of the world. A list of reference numbers are provided in Dr. Raghavan's book, where he lists 123 Gitas with sources.

  • gItam
    • ITX | PDF | GIF | UNIC - yogagItA (need to encode, volunteer?) (Also another scan)
    • saptagIta 7 gItas from from ShrimadBhagavatam with Hindi meaning.
